steps.title: Test choice B with a table point
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($9x + 3y = 3$):
Substitute $x = -6$, $y = 65$ from the table:
$$9(-6) + 3(65) = -54 + 195 = 141$$
Since $141 \neq 3$, choice B does not work.

steps.title: Test choice A with the same point
steps.content:
Fails. Testing choice A ($9x + 3y = 141$):
Substitute $x = -6$, $y = 65$:
$$9(-6) + 3(65) = -54 + 195 = 141$$ ✓

That matches choice A — A is correct.
